title: Char Siu-Style Glazed Chicken

subs.name: pork belly strips if available, or paneer steaks for a vegetarian glaze
subs.notes: this is the original char siu base for pork; paneer takes on the glaze beautifully

subs.name: 3 tbsp jaggery syrup (jaggery dissolved in 1 tbsp hot water) or sugar syrup
subs.notes: honey gives a distinct floral note; jaggery adds a deeper caramel quality

subs.name: extra 1 tbsp soy sauce + 1 tsp sugar
subs.notes: omit oyster sauce to make it shellfish-free

ingredients.name: star anise
visual_checks: gives the characteristic char siu spice note
ingredients.notes: gives the characteristic char siu spice note

subs.name: 1 tsp five-spice powder (found in Chinese grocery stores) or ½ tsp garam masala
subs.notes: five-spice is most authentic; garam masala adds warmth in a different direction

steps.text: Make the marinade: combine honey, soy sauce, oyster sauce, garlic, ginger, sugar and vinegar in a small saucepan. Add the star anise and cinnamon. Heat over medium flame for 2 minutes, stirring until the sugar dissolves. Remove from heat and cool completely.

Score the chicken thighs 2–3 times across the thickest part with a knife. Place them in a bowl and pour the cooled marinade over them. Turn to coat well. Cover and marinate in the fridge for at least 30 minutes, ideally 4 hours or overnight.
Remove the chicken from the marinade, reserving the marinade. Fish out and discard the star anise and cinnamon before using the marinade as a glaze.

Oven method: preheat the oven to 210°C. Place chicken on a foil-lined baking tray, skin-side up. Roast for 20 minutes. Brush with reserved marinade and roast for another 10 minutes until the skin is dark, sticky and caramelised. Rest for 5 minutes.

Tawa/pan method (no oven): heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a heavy skillet or tawa over medium flame. Place chicken skin-side down. Cook for 8 minutes until the skin is golden. Flip, cover with a lid and cook for 10 more minutes. Uncover, pour in 2 tablespoons of the reserved marinade and cook for 3–4 minutes, turning once, until the glaze is sticky and dark.
Slice the rested chicken and arrange on a plate. Scatter sesame seeds over the top. Serve with steamed rice and a little drizzle of the pan juices.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Verify poultry doneness with a food thermometer where possible; do not rely on colour alone.
Cook seafood until safely done for the species and preparation; keep it chilled before cooking.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: honey → 3 tbsp jaggery syrup (jaggery dissolved in 1 tbsp hot water) or sugar syrup (honey gives a distinct floral note; jaggery adds a deeper caramel quality); oyster-sauce → extra 1 tbsp soy sauce + 1 tsp sugar (omit oyster sauce to make it shellfish-free); chicken → pork belly strips if available, or paneer steaks for a vegetarian glaze (this is the original char siu base for pork; paneer takes on the glaze beautifully); star-anise → 1 tsp five-spice powder (found in Chinese grocery stores) or ½ tsp garam masala (five-spice is most authentic; garam masala adds warmth in a different direction)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: No oven? The tawa method works remarkably well — cover the chicken for the first part of cooking to ensure it cooks through, then uncover to caramelise the glaze. Honey (shahad) is available everywhere; oyster sauce and soy sauce are stocked in most Indian supermarkets. Star anise (annachi mokku) is a staple in Indian kitchens. The marinade can be prepared a day ahead and the chicken left to marinate overnight for deep flavour.
